$comment: Registers T-Hub's local MCP server with Claude Code (and any MCP client
that reads .mcp.json). The client launches `t-hub-mcp` over stdio; the
server forwards every tools/call to the RUNNING T-Hub app over a loopback
control channel it discovers from ~/.t-hub/control.json. So: the T-Hub
app must be running for the tools to do anything (the server itself starts
fine without it and reports a clear 'T-Hub is not running' tool error).
The `command` below points at the debug build produced by
`cargo build -p t-hub-mcp`. For a packaged install, point it at the
bundled `t-hub-mcp` (e.g. the Tauri sidecar binary) instead. On Windows
the control endpoint explicitly (the fast path; if that pin later goes stale
after an app restart, the server re-reads control.json and reconnects);
T_HUB_CONTROL_FILE points at a non-default handshake path.
